m_Item1.QuestionText: Which is the correct jQuery selector to select all paragraphs within <div> elements with class name "new"?
m_Item1.Explanation: div with class name new will be selected using div.new and paragraphs to be selected within that needs space separation. So it is $("div.new p")

m_Item1.QuestionText: Is jQuery a W3C standard?
m_Item1.Explanation: No. jQuery is not  a W3C standard it is a library.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Look at the following jQuery selector: $("div#intro .head"). What does it select?

Options.OptionText: All elements with class="head" inside the div element with id="intro"
All div elements with id="intro" or class="head"
The first element with id="head" inside any div element with class="intro"

m_Item1.Explanation:
Syntax : #idofelement  , .classnameofelement
So selector $("div#intro .head")  will select all elements with class="head" inside div element with id ="intro"

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will happen by $("#wblogo").html() It will return html from wblogo id element a.

Options.OptionText: It will set blank html to wblogo id element
It will return html from wblogo id element
It will set blank html to wblogo class element

m_Item1.Explanation:
If we write $("#wblogo").html("value to be set");  then it will set the value to wblogo id element.
If we write only $("#wblogo").html() then it will simply returns hetml from wblogo id element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jquery .js version file should be deployed to production?
m_Item1.Explanation: jquery-1.x.x.min.js  file should be deployed to production.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you get href value from <a href="http://www.xchanging.com" id="logo"/>
m_Item1.Explanation: Syntax :  $("#valueof_id_ attribute").attr("attributename which value we want to get.")    e.g.   $("#logo").attr("href")

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery method is used to set one or more style properties for selected elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: css() method is used to set one or more style properties for selected elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery method is used to perform an asynchronous HTTP request?
m_Item1.Explanation: jQuery.ajax() method is used to perform an asynchronous HTTP request.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which statement is true?

Options.OptionText: To use jQuery, you can refer to a hosted jQuery library at Google
To use jQuery, you must buy the jQuery library at www.jquery.com
To use jQuery, you do not have to do anything. Most browsers (Internet Explorer, Chrome, Firefox and Opera) have
the jQuery library built in the browser

m_Item1.Explanation: To use jQuery, you can refer to a hosted jQuery library at Google

m_Item1.QuestionText: What scripting language is jQuery written in?
m_Item1.Explanation: Javascript is a scripting language.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery function is used to prevent code from running, before the document is finished loading?
m_Item1.Explanation: $(document).ready() function is used to prevent code from running, before the document is finished loading.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is correct?
m_Item1.Explanation: Jquery is nothing but a javascript library.

m_Item1.QuestionText: jQuery uses CSS selectors to select elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: Yes. jQuery uses CSS selectors to select elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: With jQuery, look at the following selector: $("div"). What does it select?
m_Item1.Explanation: Syntax : $("elementName")    e.g. $("div")   it will select all div elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which sign does jQuery use as a shortcut for jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: $ sign used in jQuery as a shortcut for jQuery.

m_Item1.QuestionText: jQuery a library for client scripting or server scripting?

Options.OptionText: can be used on client as well as on server

m_Item1.Explanation: jQuery is a library for client scripting.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Is it possible to use jQuery together with AJAX?
m_Item1.Explanation: Yes. We can use Jquery with AJAX.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The jQuery html() method works for both HTML and XML documents
m_Item1.Explanation: False. jQuery html() method works for only HTML documents which will return html content of element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which event in JavaScript prevents jQuery from executing until the document is completely loaded?
m_Item1.Explanation: In jQuery, the $(document).ready() function ensures that code inside it is executed only after the DOM (Document Object Model) is fully loaded and ready to be manipulated. This event fires when the DOM is ready but before the page contents have fully loaded, making it a suitable choice for executing jQuery code without waiting for images and other resources to be loaded.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you fetch the first span on the page, which has the class ‘green’ in jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: Syntax :   $('tagname.classname:occurrence no.')  i.e.  $('span.green:first')

m_Item1.QuestionText: Look at the following jQuery selector: $('div#intro  .head'). What does it select?

Options.OptionText: The first element with id='head' inside any div element with class='intro'
All elements with class='head' inside the first div element with id='intro'
All div elements with id='intro' or class='head'
All elements with class 'head' occuring in element div with id as 'intro'

m_Item1.Explanation: space is used for finding child elements in parent. e.g $("div p") will select all p elements in div element

m_Item1.QuestionText:
var ps = $('p');
ps will be..
m_Item1.Explanation: ps is nothing but a jQuery object.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
$(document).ready(function(){});
Why do we place all jQuery methods inside this code?

Options.OptionText: It shows where jQuery starts and ends
To enable the DOM to load jQuery
To prevent jQuery code from running before the document is fully loaded

m_Item1.Explanation: Once the document or page is loaded then jQuery code needs to run.So we place all jQuery methods inside the above code.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which statement is true?

Options.OptionText: To use jQuery, you must buy the jQuery library at www.jquery.com
To use jQuery, you do not have to do anything. Most browsers (Internet Explorer, Chrome, Firefox and Opera) have the jQuery library built in the browser
To use jQuery, you can refer to a hosted jQuery library at Google
jQuery functions are available by installing plugin in the browser

m_Item1.Explanation: jQuery is free available , we just need to refer a hosted jQuery library at Google .

m_Item1.QuestionText: With jQuery, look at the following selector: $('div.intro'). What does it select?

Options.OptionText: The first div element with id='intro'
The first div element with class='intro'

m_Item1.Explanation: $('div.intro')  selects all div elements with class='intro'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is correct about jQuery selector?

Options.OptionText: A jQuery Selector is a function which makes use of expressions to find out matching elements from a DOM based on the given criteria
jQuery selectors are used to select one or more HTML elements using jQuery.
jQuery selectors start with the dollar sign and parentheses - $()

m_Item1.Explanation: JQuery selector allows to select few elements from HTML document based on certain criteria

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery selector selects all elements available in a DOM?
m_Item1.Explanation: '*' is used for selecting all elements in the current HTML page

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery function is used to prevent code from running, before the document is finished loading?
m_Item1.Explanation: document's ready() method is used to avoid any execution before entire document gets loaded in browser window

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Look at the following jQuery selector: $("div#intro .head")
What does it select?

Options.OptionText: The first element with id="head" inside any div element with class="intro"
All elements with class="head" inside the first div element with id="intro"
All div elements with id="intro" or class="head"

m_Item1.Explanation: single space is used to specify the child selection having class as "head" in the parent which is a div element having id as "intro". # symbol is used for selection on the basis of id and . symbol is used for selection on the basis of class.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery selector selects elements with the given element tag-name?
m_Item1.Explanation:
To select element by its tag name then
Syntax: $('tag-name')    e.g.   $('div'),  $('p')  etc.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery selector selects element with the given element id some-id?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Syntax : $('#idofelement')    if we want to search element by id
e.g $('#some-id')    here id name is some-id  will selects the element which having id as a 'some-id'

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery selector select elements whose css class is someclass?
m_Item1.Explanation:
In case we wants to search select element by class name then we need to use $('.className')
e.g. $('.some-class')      here class name is some-class.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does $(selector).focus() do?

Options.OptionText: Sets keyboard focus to the element

m_Item1.Explanation: .focus() sets focus to form elements like input or textarea.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the .hover() method in jQuery do?
m_Item1.Explanation: .hover() is shorthand for handling both mouseenter and mouseleave events.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which out of the following is not valid jquery event method related to key related events?
m_Item1.Explanation: keydown, keyup, keypress are valid jquery event methods but keyenter is not valid.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following can not be done by jquery functions?
m_Item1.Explanation: All tasks of Javascript can be eased with jquery functionality like AJAX calls, DOM manipulation etc.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct way to include jQuery in an HTML page?
m_Item1.Explanation: jQuery is a JavaScript library, so it must be included using a <script> tag.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is jQuery primarily used for in web development?
m_Item1.Explanation: jQuery is a JavaScript library that simplifies HTML document traversing, event handling, animating, and Ajax interactions for speedy client-side web development.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is the correct syntax for hiding an element using jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The correct syntax to hide an element using jQuery is $('.element').hide(). This applies a CSS display property of 'none' to the selected element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the main purpose of using the $(document).ready() function in jQuery?

Options.OptionText: To execute code after the DOM is fully loaded
To fetch data from the server

m_Item1.Explanation: $(document).ready() ensures that the function runs after the document is fully loaded, making it safe to manipulate DOM elements without encountering errors.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following methods is used to perform asynchronous HTTP requests in jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The $.ajax() method is used to perform an asynchronous HTTP request, providing several options such as processing the server response upon success, error, etc.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In jQuery, what is the purpose of the 'this' keyword inside a selector or event?

Options.OptionText: It refers to the global window object
It refers to the currently iterated object
It is an alias for the last used variable

m_Item1.Explanation: Within jQuery, 'this' always refers to the current element in event handlers or iteration block, allowing easy manipulation of elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you iterate over a group of elements with a specific class using jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The $('.classname').each() method is used to iterate over a group of elements selected via a specific class or selector in jQuery.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery function is used to stop the currently running animation?
m_Item1.Explanation: The stop() function is used in jQuery to stop the currently running animation on the selected element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What version of jQuery is included in the jQuery UI?

Options.OptionText: A newer version than the latest jQuery version
The same version that the developer installs separately

m_Item1.Explanation: jQuery UI includes the version that matches the version of jQuery you choose to install. It’s up to the developer to ensure compatibility.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method is used to change the HTML content of an element with jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The html() method is used in jQuery to get or set the HTML content of selected elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you select elements with a class of 'highlight' only if they are inside a div using jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The selector $('div .highlight') selects all elements with class 'highlight' that are descendants of a <div> element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery selector is used to select all elements of a particular class?
m_Item1.Explanation: In jQuery, the dot (.) is used as a prefix to select all elements of a particular class.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you select the element with id 'header' in jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: In jQuery, the hash (#) is used to select an element with a specific ID.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which selector would you use in jQuery to select all <div> elements within a <section>?
m_Item1.Explanation: The space between the elements in a jQuery selector is used to select descendants of the first element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the ':first' selector do in jQuery?

Options.OptionText: Selects elements having a specific attribute.

m_Item1.Explanation: The ':first' selector in jQuery selects the first element in a set of matched elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the result of the following selector $('p:even')?
m_Item1.Explanation: The ':even' selector starts counting at 0, so it selects even-indexed elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery selector is used to select elements with a specific attribute?
m_Item1.Explanation: In jQuery, square brackets are used to select elements with a specific attribute.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you select all <a> elements with a 'title' attribute in jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The '[attribute]' selector is used to select elements with a specific attribute.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which selector will select elements that are currently hidden in jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The ':hidden' selector selects elements that are not currently visible.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In jQuery, how do you select all input elements inside a form with the class 'details'?
m_Item1.Explanation: The space in '$('form.details input')' indicates selection of all input elements inside the specified form.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery selector selects elements containing the word 'sample'?
m_Item1.Explanation: The ':contains(text)' selector is used to select elements that contain the specified text.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you select all odd list items in a <ul> element using jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The ':odd' selector in jQuery selects elements with an odd index number.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following will select an element next to a specified one in jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The '+ next' selector in jQuery selects the immediately following sibling of the specified element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you select all paragraph elements with a class that starts with 'intro' using jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The '[attribute^="value"]' selector is used to select elements with an attribute value that starts with a specified value.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What jQuery selector would you use to select all elements with an empty value attribute?
m_Item1.Explanation: The selector '[value=""]' is used to select elements that have an attribute with an empty string value.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following selects elements that are parents in jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The ':parent' selector is used to select elements that are a parent of others.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery event method is used to attach an event handler to execute when the DOM is fully loaded?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .ready() method is specifically designed to run a function once the DOM is fully loaded, ensuring that HTML elements are available for manipulation.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the jQuery .on() method?

Options.OptionText: To attach event handlers to elements, optionally allowing them to be delegated
To execute code when an element is clicked

m_Item1.Explanation: The .on() method is used to attach event handlers to elements and can also be used for event delegation.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery method can be used to prevent an event from bubbling up the DOM tree?
m_Item1.Explanation: .stopPropagation() is used to prevent event bubbling, stopping the event from being passed to ancestor elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When using the jQuery .hover() method, what two functions are typically passed as arguments?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .hover() method accepts two functions: the first is executed when the mouse enters the element, and the second when the mouse leaves.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery event is used to watch for changes in an input field value?
m_Item1.Explanation: The change event in jQuery is used to detect when the value of an input field changes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How would you prevent the default action of an event in jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: You can prevent the default action for the specified event using the preventDefault() method.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery shorthand method is used to trigger an event handler when a form is submitted?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .submit() method allows you to attach event handlers to a form submission event.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method would you use to trigger both a specified event handler and the default action associated with an event in jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .trigger() method activates a specified event handler and executes both bound handlers and the default action.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the difference between .on() and .bind() in jQuery?

Options.OptionText: There is no difference; they are synonyms
.on() is preferred for attaching multiple events and supports delegation
.bind() allows attaching events to dynamically created elements

m_Item1.Explanation: .on() is the preferred method for event attachment in jQuery because it supports event delegation and can handle multiple events at once.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery method is useful for chaining multiple animations or functions to run in sequence?
m_Item1.Explanation: .queue() method allows you to create a chain of functions to be executed sequentially.
